title: Equilateral Height PASS: S=d²e verified for 7 tuples
_fixture_purpose: Certifies element S = d²*e = d*X = D*e, Dale Pond's 25th QA element (#25 in svpwiki.com QA Elements). Dale labeled this 'Height of equilateral triangle' but geometrically S = d*(C/2) is a rectangle area (semi-major × half-base), NOT a height. The algebraic identity is exact regardless of geometric label. Three equivalent definitions verified for 7 Pythagorean directions.
source_attribution: Dale Pond, 'Quantum Arithmetic Elements', svpwiki.com, item 25: 'S = d*X = Height of equilateral triangle' — NOTE: geometric label is incorrect; S is a rectangle area d×(C/2), not a triangle height

S.meaning: Dale's 25th element; rectangle area d*(C/2); labeled 'equilateral height' on SVP wiki but this is geometrically incorrect
S.typo_note: SVP wiki calls this 'Height of equilateral triangle' but for Unity Block W=8, continuous equilateral height=8*sqrt(3)/2≈6.93, while S=4. S is actually d*X = semi-major × half-base = a rectangle area.

cross_references.name: QA Equilateral Triangle Cert
cross_references.relation: W = equilateral side; S = equilateral height

cross_references.name: QA Dale Circle Cert
cross_references.relation: P,Q,R derived from same W; S+W+P form equilateral-circle bridge

cross_references.name: QA Sixteen Identities Cert
cross_references.relation: S extends the 16 identities to 17+ (with P,Q,R = 20+)
